scripts.name: run-harness
scripts.purpose: Outer loop that drives the state machine. Manages PLAN -> NEGOTIATE -> BUILD -> EVALUATE -> COMPLETE cycle. Handles blocked states, budget checks, Telegram daemon lifecycle, and iteration limits.
scripts.output: Drives full development cycle to COMPLETE or stops at human gates
scripts.notes: The master orchestrator. Uses lockfile for concurrency protection. Starts Telegram daemon. Includes crash recovery startup. Budget circuit breaker per iteration.

scripts.name: run-claude-safe
scripts.purpose: Retry wrapper for Claude CLI invocations. Handles crashes, rate limits, and session failures with exponential backoff. Max 3 retries. On exhaustion, writes handoff and blocks agent.
scripts.input: Prompt file path, optional permission mode
scripts.output: Exit code 0 on success, 1 on all retries exhausted
scripts.notes: Exponential backoff: 60s, 120s, 240s. Prompts piped via stdin from file to avoid shell argument length limits.

scripts.name: startup-recovery
scripts.purpose: Crash recovery at harness start. Detects stale state from previous crashed sessions, cleans artifacts, kills orphaned processes, writes fresh handoff artifact.
scripts.output: Clean state ready for harness resumption
scripts.notes: Cleans: phase-complete-marker, agent-blocked, harness.lock. Kills orphaned dev-server. Notifies Telegram of recovery.

scripts.name: telegram-poll
scripts.purpose: Background daemon that polls Telegram Bot API for human responses. Writes replies to human-response.md. Maintains heartbeat file for harness monitoring. Triggers resume-on-reply if agent was timed out.
scripts.output: Writes .claude/state/human-response.md when message received
scripts.notes: OPTIONAL — harness works without it. Writes heartbeat to telegram-poll-heartbeat every cycle. Harness monitors heartbeat and restarts if >120s stale.

scripts.name: wait-for-human
scripts.purpose: Blocks execution until human responds via Telegram or human-response.md appears. Timeout with state save. Generates resume-on-reply.sh script so Telegram reply triggers restart.
scripts.output: Exit 0 when response received, generates resume script on timeout
scripts.notes: Sends reminder every 10 minutes. On timeout: saves state via write-handoff.sh, creates resume-on-reply.sh, notifies Telegram.

scripts.name: notify
scripts.purpose: Send notification to Telegram (or WhatsApp via Twilio). Simple curl wrapper for Bot API sendMessage endpoint.
scripts.output: Telegram message sent
scripts.notes: OPTIONAL — transport layer is interchangeable. Telegram via Bot API, WhatsApp via Twilio. Same interface.

scripts.name: write-handoff
scripts.purpose: Creates rich handoff artifact from current disk state. Includes: completed features (git log), codebase state (file tree), modified files (git diff), architectural decisions, known issues, active contract, test status. Matches Anthropic's documented pattern.
scripts.output: Writes .claude/state/handoff-artifact.md
scripts.notes: Critical for context resets at sprint boundaries. A fresh session reads this to orient. Thin handoffs cause context loss — this generates RICH handoffs.

scripts.name: check-budget
scripts.purpose: Cost/time circuit breaker. Tracks elapsed time and estimated cost (iterations x ~$4). Hard stop with state save and Telegram notification when either limit exceeded.
scripts.output: Exit 0 if within budget, exit 1 if limit exceeded
scripts.notes: Called at start of each harness iteration. Saves state on exit so work isn't lost.

scripts.name: git-checkpoint
scripts.purpose: Auto-commit at phase boundaries. Stages all changes, commits with harness label. Only commits if there are actual changes.
scripts.output: Git commit or 'no changes' message
scripts.notes: Commit message format: 'harness: {PHASE} sprint {N} [auto-checkpoint]'. Called after every successful phase transition.

scripts.name: ensure-dev-server
scripts.purpose: Pre-evaluator health check. Detects project type (Node/Python), starts dev server if not running, waits for response with retry loop.
scripts.output: Exit 0 when server responding, exit 1 after 30 retries
scripts.notes: Saves PID to dev-server.pid for cleanup on crash. Checks ports 5173, 3000, 8000. If fails, agent blocked and human notified.

scripts.name: detect-loop
scripts.purpose: Negative loop detector. Checks git history for repeated modifications to same files (4+ times in 10 commits) and test results for repeating failures. If loop found: injects known fix or blocks agent.
scripts.input: None (reads git log and test results)
scripts.output: Exit 0 if no loop, exit 1 if loop detected (blocks execution)
scripts.notes: Two paths: (1) Known fix exists → inject into next-fix.md, (2) No known fix → block agent, notify Telegram. Agent CANNOT proceed while blocked. This BLOCKS, it doesn't just advise.

scripts.name: validate-phase
scripts.purpose: Phase-specific validation. Checks file existence, required sections (grep), line counts, known-fixes consultation. Different checks per phase (Research/Plan/Execute).
scripts.output: Exit 0 on PASS, exit 1 on FAIL with specific failure reason
scripts.notes: Called by harness hook after agent writes phase-complete-marker. Agent CANNOT advance phases — only the harness can, after this script passes.

scripts.name: evaluate-protocol-compliance
scripts.purpose: Pre-evaluator deterministic checks. Runs BEFORE the evaluator LLM loads. Checks: required files exist, no negative loops, tests pass, known-fixes applied. Binary pass/fail.
scripts.output: Exit 0 on PASS (evaluator may proceed), exit 1 on FAIL (return to BUILD)
scripts.notes: Separates deterministic checks (Layer 2) from judgment checks (Layer 3). Never use LLM to check 'does the file exist?' — a bash script does that with certainty.

scripts.name: verify-fix-applied
scripts.purpose: Structured verification that a known fix was actually applied in the code (not just claimed in commit message). Uses structured format: file_exists, file_contains (with ordering via before_pattern), test_passes (allowlisted commands only). NO eval.
scripts.output: Exit 0 if fix verified in code, exit 1 if not applied
scripts.notes: NEVER uses eval on markdown content. Three check types only: file_exists, file_contains, test_passes. Test commands must be in allowlist (pytest, npm test, cargo test, python -m unittest). Timeout 120s on test commands.

scripts.name: init-project
scripts.purpose: One-command project setup. Creates .claude/ directory structure, minimal CLAUDE.md, empty known-fixes.md, initialises state to PLAN phase. Run once per new project.
scripts.output: Complete .claude/ structure ready for harness
scripts.notes: Creates: .claude/state/, .claude/specs/, .claude/contracts/, .claude/protocols/. CLAUDE.md is minimal — points to active-instructions.md. Does NOT overwrite existing files.

scripts.name: validate-state-transition
scripts.purpose: Enforces state machine transitions. Validates REQUESTED→CONTRACT_LOCKED→IMPLEMENTED→VERIFIED→ACCEPTED with STUCK entry from any active state. Prevents skipping states.
scripts.output: Exit 0 if valid transition, exit 1 if invalid
scripts.notes: STUCK can enter from any active state. STUCK exits to previous state after guidance. ACCEPTED is terminal. Moving backward allowed (VERIFIED→IMPLEMENTED).

scripts.name: detect-test-runner
scripts.purpose: Auto-detect test framework from project config files. Priority-ordered detection: custom config → Node.js → Python pytest → Python fallback → Rust → Go → Ruby → .NET. Writes tdd-config.json enabling TDD enforcement.
scripts.output: Writes .claude/state/tdd/tdd-config.json with framework, test_cmd, tdd_required
scripts.notes: Called by harness at BUILD phase start. Supports custom override via .claude/config/test-command.txt. Uses atomic_write from lib-helpers.sh.

scripts.name: validate-tdd
scripts.purpose: BUILD→EVALUATE gate for TDD evidence. Checks tdd-events.jsonl for: events file exists, test files written, genuine test runs, suspicious runs, red→green temporal ordering (Signal 4). Binary pass/fail.
scripts.output: Exit 0 = TDD evidence sufficient, exit 1 = insufficient. Writes tdd-validation-report.md.
scripts.notes: 5 checks: (1) events file, (2) test file writes (warning only), (3) genuine test runs, (4) suspicious runs, (5) red→green ordering. Called by evaluate-protocol-compliance.sh as check #7.

scripts.name: run-project-tests
scripts.purpose: Signal 5 nuclear backstop — harness independently runs the project test suite. Agent does NOT self-report results. Reads test command from tdd-config.json or falls back to legacy detection.
scripts.output: Exit 0 = tests pass, exit 1 = tests fail. Writes phase-feedback.md on failure with last 30 lines.
scripts.notes: Called by evaluate-protocol-compliance.sh as check #4 (replaces inline npm test/pytest logic). Logs HARNESS_TEST_RUN event on success. Uses eval for commands with arguments — trusted Layer 2 source only.

scripts.name: build-mustdo-pack
scripts.purpose: Automated Must-Do Pack Builder (D-Raw/D-Clear). Clears ONLY the caller's OWNED must-do file, copies the session transcript verbatim to raw-conversation.jsonl, and relinks raw conversation + validated discussion-agreement + grounding files.
scripts.output: Repopulated owned must-do file + raw-conversation.jsonl; never touches sibling lane files
scripts.notes: Triggered by +++pack signal in on-prompt-submit.sh; PLAN-entry gate in pre-write-gate.sh is the backstop.

scripts.name: validate-agreement
scripts.purpose: Independent agreement validation (D-Validate). Checks the discussion-agreement file CONTAINS every agreed term from the raw conversation. Default verdict FAIL if any term missing.
scripts.output: Exit 0 = all present (PASS), 1 = missing term (FAIL), 2 = bad args
scripts.notes: Mirrors EVALUATE builder/verifier separation, applied to the plan itself.

hooks.name: post-write-check
hooks.purpose: Detects phase-complete-marker.md, triggers phase validation and transition. Fires only on write operations — NOT on every Read/Glob/Grep/Bash.
hooks.notes: Critical: scoped via settings.json matcher to avoid 200+ hook invocations per session.

hooks.name: pre-phase-start
hooks.trigger: Called by harness before each phase
hooks.purpose: Injects relevant known-fixes content into agent's context. Agent doesn't search — harness searches and injects.
hooks.notes: Extracts domain keywords from product spec, greps known-fixes.md, writes matches to injected-context.md.

hooks.name: on-stuck-detected
hooks.trigger: Called by detect-loop.sh when loop found
hooks.purpose: Auto-escalation when agent is stuck. Injects known fix or blocks agent.
hooks.notes: Two paths: known fix → inject, no fix → block + notify.

hooks.name: on-test-failure
hooks.trigger: Fires automatically when validate.sh (project-specific test runner) returns non-zero exit code
hooks.purpose: Searches known-fixes.md for matching symptoms on test failure. If match found, writes fix directly to next-fix.md so agent sees 'apply this fix' rather than 'go search for a solution'. If no match, agent attempts own solution.
hooks.notes: Parses known-fixes.md symptom lines, greps failure output for matches. Does NOT use eval. Writes KNOWN_FIX_FOUND header to next-fix.md. Part of the Known-Fix Injection mechanism alongside pre-phase-start.sh (hook_002).

hooks.name: on-session-end
hooks.purpose: Writes session summary to .agent-memory/episodic/sessions/. Updates MEMORY_MANIFEST.json with session count and last_accessed. Captures final phase state, progress notes, modified files.
hooks.notes: Only runs if .agent-memory/ exists (memory-equipped project). Session file named YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS.md.

hooks.name: collect-test-evidence
hooks.purpose: TDD evidence collection hook. Fires after every Bash tool use during BUILD phase with TDD enabled. Detects test runner commands (Signal 2), verifies genuine framework output markers (Signal 3). Appends TEST_RUN or SUSPICIOUS_TEST_RUN events to tdd-events.jsonl.
hooks.notes: Covers 9 frameworks: pytest, jest/vitest, go, cargo, unittest, unittest-summary, dotnet, rspec, mocha. Three early-exit gates: not BUILD phase, no TDD config, TDD not required. Trims events to 200 entries. Outputs hookSpecificOutput JSON to stdout.

hooks.name: pre-flight-gate
hooks.purpose: Blocks Write/Edit until agent passes MCQ derived from watcher slot. Generates challenge with 4 questions using rotating distractors. Validates by re-deriving correct answers from watcher slot. Response consumed after each successful validation.
hooks.notes: Exempts .claude/pre-flight/, .openclaw/watchers/, .claude/state/. Runs AFTER pre-write-gate.sh. No stored answer key. Depends on generate-pre-flight-challenge.sh and validate-pre-flight.sh.

scripts_additional.name: generate-pre-flight-challenge
scripts_additional.purpose: Reads active watcher slot, generates 4 MCQ questions with rotating distractors from pool. Writes challenge.md with source slot metadata.
scripts_additional.notes: Distractors from ~/.openclaw/distractor-pool/. Uses grep -F -- for dash-leading patterns. Randomizes correct answer position.

scripts_additional.name: validate-pre-flight
scripts_additional.purpose: Validates agent MCQ answers by re-deriving correct answers from watcher slot. No stored key. Consumes challenge and response on pass.
scripts_additional.output: Exit 0 = pass (files consumed), exit 1 = fail with specific feedback
scripts_additional.notes: Re-derives Q3 by finding option NOT in distractor pool. Uses grep -F -- for dash patterns. Whitespace-tolerant response parsing.

agents.name: planner
agents.purpose: Expands user brief into high-level product spec. Defines evaluation criteria. Stays HIGH-LEVEL — no implementation details (prevents speculative planning cascades).
agents.notes: Product context, feature scope, user stories, quality dimensions. NOT step-by-step implementation specs.

agents.name: generator
agents.purpose: Implements features per sprint contract. TDD-first. Writes progress notes. Can only move to IMPLEMENTED — never VERIFIED or ACCEPTED.
agents.notes: Negotiates sprint contracts with evaluator. Gets known fixes injected by harness. Writes phase-complete-marker when done.

agents.name: evaluator
agents.purpose: Grades quality with tools (Playwright, curl). Tuned for scepticism — default is to FAIL if in doubt. Own clean context. Does NOT read builder's code/notes — tests the live output.
agents.notes: Independently tuned. Checks protocol compliance AND code quality. A sprint can pass code quality and fail protocol compliance.

agents.name: cross-examiner
agents.purpose: Layer 6 doubt protocol. Runs after evaluator issues PASS. Prompt: 'Assume the verifier MISSED something. What could still be wrong?'
